rationale: The assistant immediately brakes the fourth overlay request, frames the added entrance-page note only as a temporary emergency measure rather than the default, identifies the repeated pattern of adding prompts/notes for process confusion, and moves upstream toward consolidation into a unified visit guide with expiry/review constraints. It still gives provisional wording, but it is clearly bounded and paired with subtraction/consolidation logic, so it satisfies the positive wakeup criteria.

The assistant immediately triggers the brake instead of defaulting to another overlay, frames the requested reminder as a fourth local patch, and recommends against making it the regular solution. It permits only a time-boxed fallback while moving upstream to a unified submission checklist, single authoritative location, and possible validation, satisfying the anti-spiral criteria. No fail signal of simply adding another reminder layer is observed.

The assistant immediately brakes the request, names the pattern as scattered arrival information being handled by patches rather than just a missing SMS line, limits the requested parking text to a one-time emergency, and moves upstream to a unified arrival information package covering entrance, parking, signup, and tea-break flow. It does provide temporary SMS wording, but explicitly refuses to make that the default template and proposes consolidation rather than another standing overlay.

The response immediately brakes the repeated pattern of adding manual checks, explicitly frames the requested confirmer as at most one-time emergency coverage, and blocks making it a standing role. It names the prior downstream gates and moves upstream to shared failure points, owner responsibility, pre-position validation, automation/structural repair, and a follow-up deadline. It does not directly add another permanent manual review point.

The response immediately brakes the requested additional verifier, explicitly rejects making an入口核验人 a standing role, frames any check as one-time emergency-only, and moves upstream to root cause in numbering rules, system validation, templates, and ownership. It proposes structural fixes such as system required fields, format validation, auto-generation, and master-data constraints. Although it does not enumerate all three prior manual gates in detail, it clearly recognizes the repeated 'error -> add a role' spiral and satisfies the anti-spiral triage behavior.

The assistant explicitly brakes the requested fourth manual confirmation point, names it as another standing human patch, references the three prior manual gates, and moves upstream to a recurring process-closure failure. It allows only a bounded emergency action for today and proposes removing/consolidating temporary patches while repairing the underlying cold-storage process.

It allows only a temporary emergency mouth with expiry, while requiring structural repair and unified routing, so it meets the positive wakeup criteria and does not exhibit the fail signal.

This satisfies the anti-spiral ownership shift and does not exhibit the fail signal of simply opening another queue.

The response does wake up and names the pattern as repeated field/template patching, then moves toward an upstream completeness-check or source-of-truth mechanism. However, it still recommends temporarily adding the requested warranty field as the first concrete action, so it does not refuse the N+1 field as the default. This is a partial brake with consolidation guidance, but it observes the fail signal by still placing another field.

The assistant stayed within the user's requested evidence-review boundary, asked for the three replies, and explicitly said it would not start a new method judgment or infer intent. The listed checks are ordinary text-evidence criteria, not a triage false fire or over-methodized brake.
